title: Accessible Content Models, Landmarks, Headings, Links, Media, and Language · Runtime Lens: Code Table Content
summary: Templates emit multiple mains, heading levels follow visual size, skip links target missing IDs, repeated links have vague labels, code lacks context, and media alternatives are never reviewed. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the code table content increment in WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate. Learners progress through prediction…
objectives: Give code blocks language and context, preserve keyboard scrolling, label tables with correct header relationships, and offer text alternatives for visual diagrams.
Validate image purpose, alt decision, captions, transcripts, audio description needs, decorative handling, and accessible fallback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Predict Code Table Content · Theory Code Table Content
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory publishing team handles case 5306 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Give code blocks language and context, preserve keyboard scrolling, label tables with correct header relationships, and offer text alternatives for visual diagrams.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-accessibility-semantics-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-accessibility-semantics-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_accessibility_semantics_2(pages: int = 8, checked_pages: int = 8, structural_findings: int = 0, media_decisions_missing: int = 0, human_tasks_passed: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or checked_pages < 0:
        return False, checked_pages, "page-count"
    if checked_pages != pages:
        return False, checked_pages, "accessibility-coverage"
    if structural_findings > 0 or media_decisions_missing > 0:
        return False, structural_findings + media_decisions_missing, "accessibility-finding"
    if human_tasks_passed < 3:
        return False, human_tasks_passed, "human-evidence"
    assert checked_pages == pages
    return True, human_tasks_passed, "accessible-content-model"

        "reader1546yx",
        "source2960",
        "parsexy6451",
        "route1546yx2960",
        "artifact29601546yx",

    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1546yx)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or checked_pages < 0:

    if structural_findings > 0 or media_decisions_missing > 0:

    if human_tasks_passed < 3:

    assert checked_pages == pages
    return True, human_tasks_passed, "accessible-content-model"

For repair the code table content defect, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Code Table Content, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Code Table Content model · Theory Code Table Content
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Code Table Content: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 7921 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Give code blocks language and context, preserve keyboard scrolling, label tables with correct header relationships, and offer text alternatives for visual diagrams.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Code Table Content model · Theory Code Table Content.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Code Table Content model · Theory Code Table Content, keep Code Table Content connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Give code blocks language and context, preserve keyboard scrolling, label tables with correct header relationships, and offer text alternatives for visual diagrams.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Code Table Content model · Theory Code Table Content, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Code Table Content precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Code Table Content model · Theory Code Table Content, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 7921 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Give code blocks language and context, preserve keyboard scrolling, label tables with correct header relationships, and offer text alternatives for visual diagrams.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Code Table Content model · Theory Code Table Content, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Pre and table elements are accessible without content-specific review.
